page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-1 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance powered by a single dedicated physical core on an ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. Operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz, this instance features 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor architecture includes 64 KB of L1 instruction/data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography, though it lacks advanced vector extensions such as AVX or AMX. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the t2a-standard-1 delivers average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1783.48. However, due to its single-vCPU configuration, multi-core performance scales poorly, matching the single-core output and placing it in the bottom decile of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also limited, recording 70.86 GB/sec for small cached blocks and dropping to 11.99 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic and database operations are constrained, with a Passmark CPU score of 766.57 and Redis SET operations reaching 1.27 million operations per second.

From a resource-density perspective, this instance offers a highly cost-efficient entry point into ARM64 cloud computing for workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. The primary tradeoff is the strict single-threaded bottleneck and low memory throughput, which makes it unsuitable for heavy compilation, database hosting, or large-scale LLM inference. It is best utilized for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic static web serving, simple scripting, and development or testing environments optimized for the ARM64 architecture.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-1 is an entry-level, ARM-based general-purpose instance featuring 1 dedicated vCPU on a 3.0 GHz ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor and 4.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU acceleration, this server is designed for lightweight workloads. Benchmarks show average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1783.48, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower tiers due to the single-core limitation. The instance supports AES cryptography but lacks AVX and AMX instruction sets. It represents a cost-efficient option for ARM64 development, basic static web serving, and simple single-threaded scripting tasks where resource density and low overhead are prioritized over high-throughput parallel computing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-1 is a single-vCPU ARM64 server with 4 GB RAM, offering a cost-efficient entry point for lightweight single-threaded workloads and ARM development.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-1 is a 1-vCPU ARM64 server with 4 GB RAM, designed for basic web serving and ARM development.
tagline: A dedicated single-core ARM64 server designed for lightweight workloads, basic web serving, and cost-efficient application development.
bullet_points: Single dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 core running at 3.0 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average single-core performance but limited multi-threaded scaling
No bundled local storage or GPU acceleration
Fitted for lightweight ARM64 development, testing, and basic web serving
